channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Zelenskyy meeting UK and NATO leaders as he seeks support for Ukraine's peace plan
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy arrived at 10 Downing St. in London on Thursday for talks with the leaders of Britain and NATO on his “victory plan” for the war against Russia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The Latest: Hurricane Milton weakens to Category 1, but danger remains in Florida
item.description: Hurricane Milton made landfall Wednesday along Florida’s Gulf Coast as a Category 3 storm, bringing powerful winds, deadly storm surge and potential flooding to much of the state. Milton drew fuel from exceedingly warm Gulf of Mexico waters, twice reaching Category 5 status.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Hurricane Milton spawns destructive, deadly tornadoes before making landfall
item.description: Multiple powerful tornadoes ripped across Florida hours before Hurricane Milton made landfall Wednesday, tearing off roofs, overturning vehicles and sucking debris into the air as the black V-shaped columns moved through. Deaths were reported in St.
item.dc:creator: SUMAN NAISHADHAM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Turkey's navy starts to evacuate its citizens from Beirut as Israel battles Hezbollah
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Over 2,000 Turkish citizens and some foreign nationals started boarding a Turkish military ship late Wednesday that brought in aid and will take them out of a country being hit by Israeli airstrikes targeting Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: MEHMET GUZEL and ROBERT BADENDIECK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A New York village known for its majestic mute swans faces a difficult choice after one is killed
item.description: The upstate New York village of Manlius is known for the majestic white swans that have inhabited a village pond for more than 100 years.
item.dc:creator: Carolyn Thompson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What to know about Hurricane Milton as it slams Florida's Gulf Coast
item.description: Hurricane Milton slammed into Florida as a Category 3 storm Wednesday, bringing powerful winds, heavy rain and tornadoes to much of the Gulf Coast, including communities already battered by deadly Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Taylor Swift donates $5 million to Hurricane Helene and Milton relief efforts
item.description: “This contribution will help communities rebuild and recover, providing essential food, clean water, and supplies to people affected by these devastating storms," Feeding America CEO Claire Babineaux-Fontenot said.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KTLA 5

item.title: Watch: Massive 28-foot wave captured inside Hurricane Milton
item.description: Drone footage captured a monstrous wave inside Hurricane Milton as the powerful storm passed through the Gulf of Mexico on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KRON4

item.title: NASA video shows Hurricane Milton from space
item.description: Video taken from the International Space Station puts the massive size of Hurricane Milton into perspective.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Health insurance costs up 7% for employers: Survey
item.description: The KFF study also found just 18% of the large employers surveyed offer coverage for weight-loss drugs.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Tropical dictionary: Words to know as active hurricane season continues
item.description: Let’s dive into some of the frequently used words and phrases that are on repeat during this busy week of hurricane season.
item.dc:creator: Alex Schneider, Fox 5

item.title: Arizona Democratic Party office shared with Harris-Walz campaign shot at for third time in less than a month
item.description: The Arizona Democratic National Committee office in Tempe was targeted in a shooting over the weekend in the early morning hours, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Will we see northern lights, power grid impacts amid 'severe' geomagnetic storm?
item.description: Earth is being hit by a strong solar radiation storm event that will be followed by a geomagnetic storm, space weather officials say.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's 'God Bless the USA' Bibles printed in China, records show
item.description: Thousands of copies of former President Trump's "God Bless the USA" Bible were printed in China, according to trade records.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: COVID may increase risk of heart attack, death years after infection: study
item.description: COVID-19 could increase the risk of major cardiac events, like strokes and heart attacks, or death from any cause, according to a new study.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's 'God Bless the USA' Bibles printed in China, records show
item.description: Trump started selling the "God Bless the USA" Bibles in partnership with country musician Lee Greenwood earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Trump campaigns in 'make or break' Pennsylvania
item.description: Former President Trump barnstormed Pennsylvania on Wednesday as he and his campaign have made clear the commonwealth could make or break his chances of winning in November. Trump held rallies in President Biden’s hometown of Scranton and in Reading.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump-Putin ties are back in the spotlight after new book describes calls
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A new book's assertion that former President Donald Trump may have had as many as seven private phone calls with Russian President Vladimir Putin since leaving the White House has refocused attention on their politically fraught relationship and on Trump's sustained dialogue with w
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tropical dictionary: Words to know as active hurricane season continues
item.description: Let’s dive into some of the frequently used words and phrases that are on repeat during this busy week of hurricane season.
item.dc:creator: Alex Schneider, NewsNation

item.title: Resurfaced videos simulate apocalyptic hurricane like Milton slamming into Tampa: 'Worst-case scenario'
item.description: Two exercise videos released a decade apart depict a massive Category 5 hurricane eerily similar to Milton barreling into Tampa and leaving the area in a state of destruction.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Polluted waste from Florida's fertilizer industry is in the path of Milton's fury
item.description: As Hurricane Milton pummels Florida’s west coast with powerful winds and flooding rain, environmentalists are worried it could scatter the polluted leftovers of the state’s phosphate fertilizer mining industry and other hazardous waste across the peninsula and into vulnerable waterways.
item.dc:creator: Michael Biesecker And Jason Dearen, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jenna Fischer of 'The Office' reveals breast cancer diagnosis, recovery
item.description: "I never thought I’d be making an announcement like this but here we are," Fischer wrote in an emotional Instagram post.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, NewsNation

item.title: In remote mountain communities cut off by Helene, residents look to the skies for aid
item.description: RAMSEYTOWN, N.C. (AP) — As the Black Hawk helicopter slowly descends in Ramseytown, North Carolina, a plume of sand kicks up. When the dust settles, the sprawling sea of stones and twisted metal beams becomes clear.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nazi-looted Monet, missing for over 80 years, returned to heirs of original owners in New Orleans: FBI
item.description: A Monet artwork believed to have been lost during Nazi occupation in WWII has been returned to its rightful owners after a lengthy investigation spanning decades and continents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Human Rights Campaign launching ad blitz for Harris, other Democrats
item.description: Human Rights Campaign (HRC) and its Equality Votes political action committee are launching a multimillion-dollar digital ad blitz aimed at reaching "equality voters" for the Harris-Walz ticket in key battleground states, the group announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court appears split on Oklahoma death row inmate’s fate
item.description: The Supreme Court appeared split over an Oklahoma death row inmate’s latest bid for freedom during arguments Wednesday, leaving his fate uncertain. Richard Glossip was convicted and sentenced to death for the 1997 murder of his former boss, motel owner Barry Van Treese.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Disneyland increases prices on most theme park tickets, Magic Keys
item.description: Most park tickets increased 6% in price while annual passes, known as Magic Keys, increased anywhere between 6% to 20%.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, NewsNation

item.title: Jenna Fischer of 'The Office' reveals breast cancer diagnosis, recovery
item.description: Jenna Fischer, the actress well-known for her role as Pam on "The Office," revealed her diagnosis and recovery in a post to social media.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 5 dead after a small plane crashes on Santa Catalina Island off the Southern California coast
item.description: AVALON, Calif. (AP) — Five people were killed when a small plane crashed during takeoff from Santa Catalina Island off the Southern California coast, authorities said Wednesday. The twin-engine Beechcraft 95 crashed shortly after 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lawyers: Sean 'Diddy' Combs seeks trial next April or May on sex trafficking charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Sean ‘Diddy’ Combs wants to go to trial on sex trafficking charges next spring, lawyers for the jailed hip-hop mogul told a judge on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Plane makes emergency landing in New York City after pilot dies mid-flight
item.description: Crew members on the Turkish Airlines flight worked to revive the pilot, but he died before the plane landed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ethel Kennedy, widow of Robert F. Kennedy, suffers stroke
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The family of Ethel Kennedy, the widow of Robert F. Kennedy, says she's been hospitalized after suffering a stroke.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: The Kremlin confirmed that former President Trump while in office sent Russian President Vladimir Putin COVID-19 testing devices during the height of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: LOOK: Tornado crosses Florida interstate as Hurricane Milton churns closer
item.description: As Florida's Gulf Coast braces for the impacts of Hurricane Milton Wednesday, the storm is causing severe weather across the state.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, KRON4

item.title: Helene spurs IV fluid shortage. Milton could make it worse
item.description: Hospitals across the U.S. are feeling the impact of Hurricane Helene as experts warn of an imminent intravenous (IV) solution shortage due to the temporary shutdown of a facility in North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: Over 9 million pounds of ready-to-eat meat, poultry recalled: USDA
item.description: The products may be contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes, according to the U.S. Department of Agriculture.
item.dc:creator: Michael Reiner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fearful residents flee Tampa Bay region as Hurricane Milton takes aim at Florida coast
item.description: Fearful Florida residents streamed out of the Tampa Bay region Tuesday ahead of what could be a once-in-a-century direct hit from Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
